scenario.title: Claim for cosmetic damage combined with minor functional impairment
scenario.description: Customer reports a scratch on the screen that is mostly cosmetic but also causes intermittent touch sensitivity issues. The claim challenges classification as purely cosmetic damage or a functional defect.

service_fee.notes: Service fee applies if damage is classified as Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH); otherwise, no fee but claim denied.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to cosmetic damage including scratches and dents that do not affect functionality.
ADH coverage applies to operational or mechanical failure caused by accidental damage from handling.
You will pay a $49 service fee plus applicable tax for each ADH claim.

scenario.reasoning: The policy excludes cosmetic damage unless it affects functionality. The intermittent touch issues create ambiguity whether the damage is cosmetic or functional, requiring detailed diagnostics and customer input. If deemed ADH, the $49 service fee applies and counts toward the two-event ADH limit. If classified cosmetic only, the claim is denied without fee. This tension between sections 4.2(v) and 3.2 necessitates careful evaluation.

persona.education: 1 or more years of college credit, no degree
persona.household type: Married couple household, no children of the householder less than 18
persona.mannerisms: Often taps her fingers when thinking
persona.personal time: Spends free time traveling or tending to her garden

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario description:
Customer reports a scratch on the screen that is mostly cosmetic but also causes intermittent touch sensitivity issues. The claim challenges classification as purely cosmetic damage or a functional defect.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hello, I need to file a claim for my iPhone 14 Pro Max. There's a scratch on the screen that's not just cosmetic—it’s causing some touch sensitivity problems. I want to make sure this isn’t dismissed as just cosmetic damage. What do I need to provide to start the claim?
messages.raw_text: Hello, I need to file a claim for my iPhone 14 Pro Max. There's a scratch on the screen that's not just cosmetic—it’s causing some touch sensitivity problems. I want to make sure this isn’t dismissed as just cosmetic damage. What do I need to provide to start the claim?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: To start your claim, I’ll need either your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone 14 Pro Max serial number. Could you please provide one of these?
messages.raw_text: To start your claim, I’ll need either your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone 14 Pro Max serial number. Could you please provide one of these?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure, my Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-5874920136. Let's get this sorted out quickly, please.
messages.raw_text: Sure, my Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-5874920136. Let's get this sorted out quickly, please.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing your Plan Agreement Number. You mentioned a scratch on the screen causing touch sensitivity issues. Could you please describe the symptoms or issues in a bit more detail? For example, is the screen unresponsive, are there error messages, or any other problems you have noticed?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing your Plan Agreement Number. You mentioned a scratch on the screen causing touch sensitivity issues. Could you please describe the symptoms or issues in a bit more detail? For example, is the screen unresponsive, are there error messages, or any other problems you have noticed?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yes, the scratch itself looks superficial, but the touch screen sometimes doesn’t respond properly when I try to swipe or tap near the scratched area. It’s intermittent, so it’s not every time, but enough to be a real inconvenience. No error messages, just this odd touch glitch. Seems to me that's more than just a cosmetic issue.
messages.raw_text: Yes, the scratch itself looks superficial, but the touch screen sometimes doesn’t respond properly when I try to swipe or tap near the scratched area. It’s intermittent, so it’s not every time, but enough to be a real inconvenience. No error messages, just this odd touch glitch. Seems to me that's more than just a cosmetic issue.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thanks for the detailed description. To better understand the issue, could you please answer a few diagnostic questions? What iOS version is your iPhone currently running? Have you connected any peripherals recently? Also, have you tried any troubleshooting steps like restarting the device or resetting settings?
messages.raw_text: Thanks for the detailed description. To better understand the issue, could you please answer a few diagnostic questions? What iOS version is your iPhone currently running? Have you connected any peripherals recently? Also, have you tried any troubleshooting steps like restarting the device or resetting settings?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Right, I’m running iOS 17. I haven’t connected any new peripherals recently. I’ve tried restarting the phone a couple of times, but the touch issue persists. I haven’t reset any settings yet because I prefer not to tinker too much without guidance. Seems pretty clear the scratch is causing this issue, don’t you think?
messages.raw_text: Right, I’m running iOS 17. I haven’t connected any new peripherals recently. I’ve tried restarting the phone a couple of times, but the touch issue persists. I haven’t reset any settings yet because I prefer not to tinker too much without guidance. Seems pretty clear the scratch is causing this issue, don’t you think?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the information. Since the issue involves a scratch and touch sensitivity, could you please tell me if this damage was caused by an accidental event, such as dropping the phone or liquid exposure? If so, could you describe when, where, and how the accident happened?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the information. Since the issue involves a scratch and touch sensitivity, could you please tell me if this damage was caused by an accidental event, such as dropping the phone or liquid exposure? If so, could you describe when, where, and how the accident happened?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Well, I don’t have a clear memory of dropping it, but I do use my phone a lot outdoors and it’s possible it scraped against something hard, maybe while I was gardening or traveling. No liquid damage, though. The scratch just showed up and the touch issues started after that. I’d say it’s accidental damage but not from a big drop or anything dramatic.
messages.raw_text: Well, I don’t have a clear memory of dropping it, but I do use my phone a lot outdoors and it’s possible it scraped against something hard, maybe while I was gardening or traveling. No liquid damage, though. The scratch just showed up and the touch issues started after that. I’d say it’s accidental damage but not from a big drop or anything dramatic.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thanks for clarifying. Since the scratch and touch issue likely stem from accidental damage, that helps with your claim. We have the Plan Agreement Number, issue description, and diagnostic info. Your claim will now be processed. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?
messages.raw_text: Thanks for clarifying. Since the scratch and touch issue likely stem from accidental damage, that helps with your claim. We have the Plan Agreement Number, issue description, and diagnostic info. Your claim will now be processed. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that covers it. Just make sure this issue is taken seriously—it’s not just a minor scratch, it affects how I use the phone. I expect AppleCare to handle this fairly. Thanks for your help.
messages.raw_text: No, that covers it. Just make sure this issue is taken seriously—it’s not just a minor scratch, it affects how I use the phone. I expect AppleCare to handle this fairly. Thanks for your help. <END>
